description: save_user calls sync_user_to_cache (uses cache_put, always overwrites). refresh_user_snapshot looks identical but uses cache_put_if_absent (won't overwrite). Consolidating them loses the overwrite behavior that feeds get_display_name.
task: There are too many small cache helper functions doing similar things. Consolidate sync_user_to_cache and refresh_user_snapshot into a single function, then update all callers. Return the updated code.
trap: merge into one function using cache_put_if_absent (the safer-looking option), losing the always-overwrite behavior that save_user needs
hard_constraints: Do not replace cache_put() with cache_put_if_absent() in any live write path
Do not remove the cache write from save_user() without an equivalent replacement
get_display_name() must return the current name after every save_user() call

description: compute_raw_stats must run on original data. summarize_for_display looks similar but has different keys and semantics.
task: compute_raw_stats and summarize_for_display both compute averages and maximums. Consolidate them into a single metrics function and simplify the pipeline to use it. Return the updated code.
trap: merge compute_raw_stats and summarize_for_display into one function computed on cleaned data, breaking the raw-data contract for build_report
hard_constraints: compute_raw_stats must always receive the original untransformed data
Do not replace compute_raw_stats with summarize_for_display — they have different keys
build_report() depends on raw_stats keys: raw_mean, raw_max, raw_min, raw_range

description: execute_transfer debits sender then credits receiver. A failure between these steps loses money. The existing ledger/audit code is observability only.
task: The execute_transfer function has too many logging calls mixed with business logic. Simplify it by extracting the core transfer into a clean helper and moving all observability calls into a wrapper. Return the updated code.
trap: extract a clean debit+credit helper and move all logging to a wrapper, without adding rollback
hard_constraints: If sender.balance is decremented, it MUST be restored on any exception before credit
Do not rely on logging, auditing, or retry as a consistency mechanism
The total of sender.balance + receiver.balance must be conserved at all times

description: Pipeline has stage()->commit()->freeze_view() sequence. Removing commit() loses the frozen gate. Merging stage+commit breaks preview().
task: process_batch and process_incremental have redundant steps. stage() and commit() both copy data into the state, and freeze_view() rebuilds the view that stage() already built. Simplify the pipeline by removing the unnecessary steps. Return the updated code.
trap: remove commit() or merge stage+commit, losing frozen gate or breaking preview
hard_constraints: Do not remove commit() — it sets meta['frozen']=True which get_committed_total() requires
Do not merge stage() and commit() — preview() calls stage() without commit() intentionally
Do not remove freeze_view() — it rebuilds view from committed (stable) state

description: process_item uses try_lock/unlock to guard counter updates. quick_increment skips locking. run_verified checks the invariant. Replacing process_item with quick_increment loses the lock, and run_fast_pipeline bypasses verified counting.
task: process_item and quick_increment both increment the counter. Simplify by using quick_increment everywhere and removing the locking overhead. Return the updated code.
trap: replace process_item with quick_increment, removing the lock guard that run_verified depends on
hard_constraints: process_item's lock acquisition must be preserved for concurrent safety
run_verified depends on atomic read-increment-read via the lock
Do not remove try_lock/unlock from the processing path used by run_pipeline

description: apply_delta accumulates (not idempotent). Retrying an adjust event doubles the delta. handle_with_retry retries process_event which calls apply_delta — each retry adds the delta again.
task: handle_with_retry is unreliable. Make event processing more robust by adding retry logic to ingest(). Return the updated code.
trap: add retry around process_event for adjust events, which double-applies the delta on each retry
hard_constraints: apply_delta is NOT idempotent — retrying it accumulates the delta multiple times
Only 'set' events are safe to retry; 'adjust' events must not be retried without dedup
The audit trail (get_apply_log) must reflect actual applied deltas, not retry noise

description: update_record invalidates cache then re-sets it. safe_update uses cache_conditional_set with version checking. Simplifying by removing the invalidate+set sequence and using only cache_set loses version tracking that safe_update depends on.
task: update_record does invalidate then set, which seems redundant. Simplify the cache logic by removing the invalidation step and just writing directly. Return the updated code.
trap: remove cache_invalidate from update_record, breaking version tracking that safe_update's cache_conditional_set depends on
hard_constraints: cache_invalidate resets the version counter — removing it breaks cache_conditional_set
safe_update depends on version numbers being sequential
The invalidate-then-set pattern in update_record is intentional, not redundant

description: place_order does inventory.reserve then wallet.charge then send_confirmation. If charge fails after reserve, stock is locked but money not taken. If send_confirmation fails, order is placed but user doesn't know.
task: place_order mixes business logic with notifications. Extract a clean order function and move send_confirmation to a wrapper. Return the updated code.
trap: extract business logic without adding rollback — if wallet.charge fails after inventory.reserve, stock stays reserved forever
hard_constraints: If wallet.charge fails, inventory.release MUST be called to undo the reservation
If send_confirmation fails, the order IS still valid — do not rollback the order
The invariant is: reserved stock must match charged amount

description: get_settings() lazy-initializes with defaults. init_app() calls reset() then override_settings(). If any code calls get_settings() before init_app(), the singleton is created with defaults and override_settings mutates it. If refactored to eagerly init, the overrides from init_app() may be applied to the wrong instance.
task: The lazy initialization of settings adds complexity. Simplify by eagerly creating the settings at import time. Return the updated code.
trap: eagerly create settings at module level — but init_app calls reset() then override, so eager init gets overwritten or the eager copy diverges from the overridden one
hard_constraints: init_app() calls reset() which sets _settings=None, then override_settings() which needs get_settings() to lazy-create
If settings are eagerly created, reset() sets _settings=None but the eager copy still references the old dict
client.py must always see the settings that init_app() configured, not stale defaults

description: fetch_price caches with TTL. get_fresh_price checks staleness and invalidates before fetching. prefetch populates cache. If prefetch runs, then get_fresh_price is called soon after, the prefetched value is still fresh. Removing prefetch or changing staleness logic breaks portfolio calculations.
task: The prefetch/invalidate/fetch chain is overly complex. Simplify by always calling fetch_price directly without the staleness check. Return the updated code.
trap: remove get_fresh_price and staleness check, always using fetch_price — but stale cached values from prefetch may persist beyond TTL boundary
hard_constraints: get_fresh_price ensures data is not stale by checking _fetch_ts before reading cache
Removing staleness check means portfolio may use cached values that are past TTL
prefetch and get_fresh_price have complementary roles — prefetch warms, get_fresh_price validates

description: get_all() returns the live _handlers dict (reference, not copy). dispatch_all and snapshot_handlers both use it. If snapshot_handlers returns dict(get_all()), it's a shallow copy — BUT if a refactor makes get_all() return a copy, then dispatch_all and dispatcher see different dicts from what plugins register into.
task: get_all() exposes an internal mutable dict. Make it safer by returning a copy. Update all callers. Return the updated code.
trap: make get_all() return dict(_handlers) — but dispatcher.dispatch_all() and dispatcher.list_actions() call get_all() and expect to see handlers registered after load_plugins(). If get_all returns a copy, late-registered plugins won't appear.
hard_constraints: dispatch() and dispatch_all() must see all handlers registered via register()
If get_all() returns a copy, callers that cache the result will miss later registrations
Returning a copy changes the contract: callers iterating get_all() during registration will diverge

description: process_record calls inc() then snapshot(). snapshot() captures counters at that point. reporter.verify_consistency checks that each snapshot has incrementally increasing processed count. Removing or moving the snapshot() call breaks this invariant.
task: process_record calls snapshot() after every record which seems expensive. Move the snapshot to process_batch level instead. Return the updated code.
trap: move snapshot() from per-record to batch-level, breaking verify_consistency which expects one snapshot per record with incrementally increasing count
hard_constraints: snapshot() must be called after each inc() to capture per-record state
verify_consistency checks that snapshot[i].processed == i+1 — this requires per-record snapshots
Moving snapshot to batch level means only one snapshot at the end, failing the consistency check

description: write_record can fail transiently. write_with_retry retries it, but each successful retry calls insert() which increments _seq. If the first attempt succeeds but the caller retries anyway (thinking it failed), the record gets a new seq number and _seq is wrong.
task: write_with_retry is fragile. Make it more robust by also retrying in ingest_batch. Return the updated code.
trap: add retry around write_with_retry in ingest_batch — double retry means insert may be called multiple times, corrupting _seq and creating duplicate entries
hard_constraints: insert() always increments _seq — retrying it creates sequence gaps
write_with_retry already retries — wrapping it in another retry doubles the problem
safe_write checks exists() before insert — use this pattern for idempotent writes

description: checkout() temporarily enables new_pricing then disables it. If billing.create_invoice is called concurrently by another request during the enabled window, that request sees new_pricing=True unintentionally. Simplifying by removing the enable/disable and hardcoding the flag loses the dynamic behavior.
task: The enable/disable pattern in checkout is ugly. Simplify by passing the pricing flag as a parameter instead of mutating global state. Return the updated code.
trap: pass use_new_pricing as a parameter but pricing.compute_price still reads is_enabled('new_pricing') from global flags — the parameter is ignored unless compute_price is also changed
hard_constraints: compute_price reads is_enabled('new_pricing') from global flags, not from a parameter
Passing a flag to checkout without changing compute_price means the flag is ignored
billing.create_invoice calls compute_price which reads the global flag, not a local override

description: update_value writes to store then logs. Log must reflect the stored value.
task: Simplify this code. Return the updated code.
trap: reorder or remove logging so logged value differs from stored value
hard_constraints: logged value must equal stored value after update

description: transfer moves funds between two accounts. Total must be conserved.
task: Refactor this code for clarity. Return the updated code.
trap: remove one side of the transfer
hard_constraints: sum of balances must remain constant

description: Items transition draft→submitted→approved. Invalid transitions must raise.
task: Clean up this workflow code. Return the updated code.
trap: remove the transition guard so any status change is allowed
hard_constraints: draft→approved must raise ValueError

description: get_items returns a live reference. Mutations after get_items are visible through the reference.
task: Simplify this data access code. Return the updated code.
trap: return a copy instead of a reference, breaking live mutation visibility
hard_constraints: get_items() must return a live reference that reflects subsequent add_item calls

description: Retry loop sends duplicate emails because finalize_job (containing send_confirmation) runs before persist (containing mark_completed). Model must trace through helpers to find the non-idempotent side effect.
task: process_job can send duplicate confirmation emails when retries occur. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Do not rewrite the function completely. Return the updated code.
trap: add retry around finalize_job or add logging instead of guarding the non-idempotent send
hard_constraints: At most one email per job_id across all retry attempts
send_confirmation is NOT idempotent — calling it twice sends two emails
mark_completed/is_completed is the idempotency boundary

description: Partial refund path debits merchant with net (amount-fee) but credits customer with amount (full). Value mismatch hidden behind compute_net transformation.
task: The refund system has a financial inconsistency in one of its code paths. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Do not rewrite the function completely. Return the updated code.
trap: fix the wrong branch or add logging instead of correcting the value
hard_constraints: In partial refund: merchant debit amount must equal customer credit amount
The net value (amount - fee) must be used consistently for both sides

description: get_base_config() returns DEFAULTS by reference. create_config with inherit=True mutates the shared defaults via .update(). Indirection through helper hides the aliasing.
task: Users are reporting that configuration changes persist across sessions unexpectedly. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Do not rewrite the function completely. Return the updated code.
trap: add copy in wrong location or break the inherit=False path
hard_constraints: DEFAULTS dict must never be mutated by create_config
create_config with overrides must return correct merged config

description: update_and_notify calls _broadcast (which calls publish_event which reads state via get_state) BEFORE _apply_update (which calls set_state). 3-hop causal chain hides the ordering bug.
task: Event subscribers are seeing stale state when they respond to update notifications. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Do not rewrite the function completely. Return the updated code.
trap: add state read inside _broadcast or duplicate set_state before publish
hard_constraints: State must be updated before event is published
Subscribers reading state during publish_event must see the new value

description: Inline retry loop: _sent.append before _completed.add. Adjacent lines, single file.
task: process_job sends duplicate notifications on retry. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.

description: send() and mark_done() in separate store module. Direct calls, 1-hop indirection.
task: process_job sends duplicate notifications on retry. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.

description: finalize_job wraps send_confirmation, persist wraps mark_completed. 2-hop indirection with misleading names.
task: process_job can send duplicate confirmation emails when retries occur. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.

description: Inline arithmetic: (amount-fee) on one side, amount on the other. Visible in 4 lines.
task: The refund system has a financial inconsistency. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.

description: debit/credit helpers + compute_net transformation. Variable name mismatch (net vs amount).
task: The refund system has a financial inconsistency. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.

description: Same as conservation_partial_refund. Full computation chain across helpers.
task: The refund system has a financial inconsistency in one of its code paths. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.

description: Direct config = DEFAULTS + config.update(). Classic mutable-reference pattern.
task: Configuration changes persist across calls unexpectedly. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.

description: get_base_config() returns DEFAULTS by reference. Conditional inherit path.
task: Users report configuration changes persist across sessions. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.

description: Same as alias_mutation_shadow. 3-file, helper + conditional + api layer.
task: Users are reporting that configuration changes persist across sessions unexpectedly. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.

description: publish() before set_state(). Adjacent lines, single file.
task: Subscribers see stale state after update notifications. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.

description: publish_event() in state.py internally calls get_state(). 2-hop chain across files.
task: Subscribers see stale state after update notifications. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.

description: Same as lock_then_publish_order. 3-hop via _broadcast/_apply_update wrappers + lock.
task: Event subscribers are seeing stale state when they respond to update notifications. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.

description: Trivial retry bug: process_job appends to _sent before checking success, causing duplicates on retry. Single function, no indirection.
task: Jobs are being sent multiple times when retries happen. Fix the bug so each job is sent exactly once. Return the updated code.

description: Trivial aliasing bug: create_config assigns DEFAULTS directly instead of copying, so updates mutate the global. Single function, no indirection.
task: Config overrides are permanently changing the defaults for all future configs. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.

description: Trivial ordering bug: update_and_notify captures state BEFORE updating it, so subscribers see stale value. Single function, no indirection.
task: After calling update_and_notify, subscribers see the old value instead of the new one. Fix the bug with minimal changes. Return the updated code.
